MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Joel B. Crane of SEDGWICK DETERT, MORAN & ARNOLD LLP (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / moving party Chevron U.S.A., Inc. U.S.A. Inc., individually, and erroneously sued and served as successor in interest, parent, alter ego and/or equitable trustee of Standard Oil Company
Christine A. Renkin of FARRISE LAW FIRM (800-748-6186), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Brenda Broussard, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Chevron U.S.A., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted in part and denied in part and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as moot as to issue 1 (premises liability) and denied as to issue 2 (conspiracy). As to Mrs. Broussard's exposure to asbestos attributable to Chevron's roofing product, summary judgment is denied. Dr. Horn's declaration creates a triable issue whether exposure to such product was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mrs. Broussard's risk of developing mesothelioma. As to Mrs. Broussard's alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to work performed by or around her husband during his employment at the Standard Oil Building, partial summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mrs. Broussard was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant related to her husband's work at the Standard Oil Building. Therefore plaintiffs' causes of action based on premises liability (the sixth, seventh, eighth, ninth, tenth, sixteenth, seventeenth, eighteenth, nineteenth and twentieth causes of action) are dismissed. Summary adjudication of issue 1 (the premises liability causes of action pursuant to Campbell v. Ford Motor Co.) is off calendar as moot pursuant to the above ruling dismissing the premises liability causes of action. Summary adjudication of issue 2 (fifth and fifteenth causes of action for conspiracy) is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to support their conspiracy causes of action. By failing to demonstrate that it propounded contention discovery designed to elicit all facts and evidence plaintiffs have to support their claims for conspiracy, and by failing to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses thereto, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Miles Lendway of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13684 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

The trial date is continued to September 16, 2013 at 11:15am in Department 503. The new trial date serves as the initial trial date for discovery purposes only as it relates to B.R. Funsten. The June 3, 2013 trial date remains the initial trial date for discovery purposes as related to all other defendants. Defendant demonstrated good cause for a continuance as required by CRC 3.1332. Order signed after the hearing. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
